Paragraph

The triangle inequality follows from the Cauchy-Schwarz inequality by
\begin{equation*} \Vert x +y \Vert^{2}=(x+y, x+y)=(x, x)+2(x, y)+(y, y) \le (x, x)+2\Vert x \Vert \Vert y \Vert +(y, y) =\left(\Vert x \Vert + \Vert y \Vert \right)^{2}. \end{equation*}
The Pythagorean relation clearly follows from the above line of proof when \((x, y)=0\text{.}\)
in-context
